summary: Sanity backend check after iteration 3 CI-only YAML fix (added `sudo ln -sfn $GITHUB_WORKSPACE /app` symlink step in both workflows). Confirmed preview backend is unaffected: health endpoint returns 200, regression smoke suite passes 30/30, and /api/changelog returns valid JSON with required keys. Both YAML files load via yaml.safe_load.
action_items: Actual CI validation must happen on GitHub by re-running the workflow there — preview cannot validate that the symlink step works on the runner. Confirm the next pushed commit triggers backend-tests.yml and prod-smoke-canary.yml to green.
critical_code_review_comments: Symlink approach `sudo ln -sfn $GITHUB_WORKSPACE /app` is a pragmatic short-term fix but couples tests to the dev-container layout. Long-term, tests should use relative paths or a PROJECT_ROOT env var to be portable across environments (CI, local dev, container).
Consider adding `if: runner.os == 'Linux'` guard on the symlink step to avoid surprises if a macOS/Windows runner is ever added.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iter 3 was CI YAML-only; no app/test/requirements changes. Preview verified: /api/health=200, regression smoke 30/30 passed in 13.3s, /api/changelog returns keys [community_shipped, engineering_log, credit_cutoff]. The CI-side validity of the symlink step itself cannot be verified from the preview environment — that requires a GitHub workflow run.
rca of the issue: N/A — sanity check only; no bug to RCA. Original CI failure (PermissionError on /app) is resolved by the symlink step which makes /app point to $GITHUB_WORKSPACE on the runner.
